text:
The task involves migrating away from `ansible.module_utils.common._collections_compat` — an internal shim that re-exports abstract base classes (ABCs) from `collections.abc` such as `Mapping`, `MutableMapping`, `Sequence`, and `MutableSequence`. 

Key findings:
1. **`lib/ansible/module_utils/common/_collections_compat.py`**: The shim itself — it re-exports ABCs from `collections.abc` and already includes a deprecation warning (`_warnings.deprecate(...)`) when imported. It imports from `ansible.module_utils.common.warnings` to issue the deprecation.
2. **`test/lib/ansible_test/_util/controller/sanity/pylint/plugins/unwanted.py`**: The pylint plugin that enforces `ansible-bad-import` and `ansible-bad-import-from` rules. It contains an `UnwantedEntry` for `collections` (with a note referencing `collections.abc`), which needs to be updated to also flag or stop recommending `_collections_compat` as the canonical import.
3. **`test/sanity/ignore.txt`**: The sanity ignore file that currently allows certain pylint bad-import violations; needs review to ensure no `_collections_compat` exceptions are present.
4. **`test/integration/targets/ansible-test-sanity/ansible_collections/ns/col/tests/integration/targets/hello/files/bad.py`**: Test file that intentionally triggers `ansible-bad-import` and `ansible-bad-import-from` for testing the pylint plugin.
5. **`test/integration/targets/ansible-test-sanity/ansible_collections/ns/col/tests/sanity/ignore.txt`**: Corresponding sanity ignore for the integration test.
6. **`lib/ansible/vars/clean.py`**: One of the controller-side files that likely imports collection ABCs and may be using the internal shim.
7. **`lib/ansible/module_utils/common/warnings.py`**: Used by the shim to issue deprecation warnings.

The migration requires: finding all internal usages of `_collections_compat`, replacing them with direct `collections.abc` imports, updating the `unwanted.py` pylint plugin to flag `_collections_compat` as a bad import, and verifying the sanity ignore files are clean.
